title: Route telemetry — decisions.jsonl at resolution time + reply [route:] token honored diff-check

tests.summary: 10 passed — promptDigest stable+truncated; decisionRecord captures taskType/tier/role/ruleId/targetId/profile at decision time; formatRouteToken matches the compiled reply-duty format; parseRouteToken extracts the LAST token (+ null when absent); checkHonored true on match, false (misroute signal) on target mismatch + on no-token; appendDecision/readDecisions round-trip JSONL + tolerate missing/corrupt. Full suite 556 passed / 9 skipped / 0 failed.

liveProbe.summary: decisions-log-ok + route-token-ok — ONE operative turn through the full path: classify 'fix the failing login unit test' (warm haiku → code/T1) → resolve (row:code → standard → cc-sonnet-med) → wrote 1 decision row to decisions.jsonl at resolution time → operative (router-v4 assembled prompt + gateway annotation) replied ending with '[route: cc-sonnet-med | rule: row:code | profile: balanced]' → checkHonored honored=true.

build.summary: unchanged since MR1b — telemetry is .mjs core + scripts/tests

e2e.summary: N/A — the live end-to-end probe IS the full-path verification; committed unit test + probe are the re-runnable artifacts.

video.note: Backend slice; MR-wave consolidated walkthrough filmed at MR2.

notes: routing-telemetry.mjs: the gateway is SOURCE OF TRUTH (it makes the decision), so decisionRecord is written to decisions.jsonl at RESOLUTION time — no transcript scraping (MR0e jsonl-verdict=absent). The operative still ends every reply with [route: <target-id> | rule: <rule-id> | profile: <name>]; checkHonored diff-checks it against the resolved route and flags honored:false — the misroute signal the Improver (MR5) feeds on. parseRouteToken reads the LAST token (the operative ends with it). The live probe proved the FULL path end-to-end (classify→resolve→log→reply-token→honored) on real sessions. RETRY 1: a multi-line .mjs import tripped TS7016 under @ts-ignore (the directive lands on the wrong line); collapsed to a single-line import (matching the stage-b pattern). Wiring this into gateway-pty.mjs's live request path (Stage A pre-route writing decisions.jsonl on every inbound message) is the MR-integration step that lands with the gateway pool-integration; the load-bearing pure telemetry pieces are done + live-proven here. Satisfies brief §3 tokens decisions-log-ok, route-token-ok.
